---
title: Young Ri Lee
image: avatar.jpg
page-layout: full
about:
  id: about
  template: trestles
  image-shape: round
  links:
  - icon: globe
    href: https://www.ewha.ac.kr/ewha/professor/info.do?mode=view&pId=0JwQAaTyz3ZtUjpr9W9duw==__;!!Mak6IKo!KjgNgifp5U3XT4aCltYkuTPwxRT6tugUbvcML_4-Za1W40Yw96A5IhJszAflnvWIDyYwcH1wGxIMmKjxVUiw1g$
  - icon: envelope
    href: mailto:yr.lee@ewha.ac.kr
  - text: '{{< ai google-scholar >}}'
    href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=ajUXozoAAAAJ&hl=en&authuser=1
org_name: Ewha Womans University
org_url: https://www.ewha.ac.kr/ewhaen/index.do
role: Assistant Professor
people-group: Alumni
listing:
  - id: publications
    contents: 
      - "../../publications/**/*.qmd"
      - "../../presentations/*.qmd"
      - "../../posts/**/*.qmd"
    sort: 
      - "date desc"
    type: table
    categories: false
    sort-ui: false
    filter-ui: false
    max-items: 50
    fields: [date, author, title]
    field-display-names:
      date: "Date"
      author: "Authors"
      title: "Title"
    include: 
      author: 'Young Ri Lee'
---

:::::{#about}

Young Ri is currently an assistant professor in the Department of Psychology, College of Social Sciences at Ewha Womans University, specializing in psychometrics and research methodology. She received her Ph.D. in Quantitative Methodology from the Department of Educational Psychology at the University of Texas at Austin and subsequently served as a postdoctoral researcher at the Institute for Urban Education at the University of Chicago and the Institute for Learning Sciences at Seoul National University. Her major research areas include the development and application of multilevel models, including cross-classified random-effects models, and meta-analysis methodologies that synthesize evidence from the social sciences. Additionally, she studies the daily learning and mental health of adolescents using intensive longitudinal methods, such as daily journals. 


::::{.grid}

::: {.g-col-6}

## Education

- PhD in Quantitative Methods | 2023 <br> [University of Texas at Austin]{style='color:gray;'}
- MA in Educational Measurement and Statistics | 2017 <br> [Korea University]{style='color:gray;'}
- BA in Education | 2014 <br> [Korea University]{style='color:gray;'}

:::

::: {.g-col-6}

## Interests

- Hierarchical Linear Modeling
- Robust Variance Estimation methods
- Meta-analysis

:::

::::

:::::

:::::{#publications}
:::::
